skills.name: audit
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "audit", "run an audit", "check quality", "review before merge", "a11y audit", or "code audit" — detects audit type (UI component, a11y, or code) and dispatches the right specialist

skills.name: auto-improvement
skills.description: (ePost) Use when reviewing session performance, analyzing improvement trends, or understanding the metrics-capture-detection pipeline

skills.name: bootstrap
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "bootstrap", "scaffold", "init a new project", "create a new module", or "set up from scratch" — initializes project structure for the detected platform

skills.name: code-review
skills.description: (ePost) Use when reviewing code, checking quality before commit, or auditing changed files for issues

skills.name: convert
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "convert this prototype", "migrate this code", "make this match our design system", or "port from external source" — transforms external or prototype code into production-ready, system-consistent code

skills.name: cook
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "implement", "build", "add a feature", "cook", "make this work", or "continue the plan" — dispatches platform-aware feature implementation for web, iOS, Android, or backend

skills.name: core
skills.description: (ePost) Use when any agent modifies files, makes architectural decisions, or needs safety and documentation rules

skills.name: data-store
skills.description: (ePost) Use when an agent needs to persist or retrieve project-level data across sessions via .epost-data/

skills.name: debug
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "debug", "trace this", "diagnose", "it crashes", "why is this failing", or provides a stack trace — investigates root cause using platform-specific debugging tools

skills.name: doc-coauthoring
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user wants to write a doc, proposal, spec, RFC, PRD, or decision record collaboratively

skills.name: docs
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "write docs", "update docs", "document this", "init docs", "reorganize documentation", or "add component docs" — detects docs intent (init, update, migrate, component) and runs the right workflow

skills.name: docs-seeker
skills.description: (ePost) Use when you need API docs, library references, or framework documentation for an unfamiliar package

skills.name: epost
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user intent is ambiguous, spans multiple workflows, or is not matched by a specific skill — performs intent detection and routes to the correct specialist (plan, cook, fix, audit, git, docs)

skills.name: error-recovery
skills.description: (ePost) Use when operations fail transiently — timeouts, network errors, retries, circuit breakers, graceful degradation

skills.name: fix
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "fix", "broken", "error", "failing", "this doesn't work", or pastes an error message — identifies error type and applies platform-appropriate fix

skills.name: get-started
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "get started", "I'm new to this project", "onboard me", "what is this codebase", or "help me understand this project" — discovers project state and delivers a structured onboarding experience

skills.name: git
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "commit", "push", "create a PR", "ship it", "done", "merge", or "open a pull request" — runs the appropriate git workflow (commit, push, or PR creation)

skills.name: infra-cloud
skills.description: (ePost) Use when working with GCP infrastructure — Terraform, Cloud Build, Cloud Run, GKE, deployment pipelines

skills.name: infra-docker
skills.description: (ePost) Use when working with Dockerfiles, docker-compose, containers, or containerized deployments

skills.name: knowledge-capture
skills.description: (ePost) Use after completing a task — capture learnings, save patterns, record post-mortems to docs/

skills.name: knowledge-retrieval
skills.description: (ePost) Use when you need prior art, past decisions, or existing patterns — checks docs/, skills, and RAG before external sources

skills.name: plan
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "plan", "design this", "architect", "spec out", "how should we build", or "create a roadmap" — produces a phased implementation plan scaled to task complexity

skills.name: problem-solving
skills.description: (ePost) Use when stuck, facing a complex bug, or need root cause analysis — 5 whys, bisection, inversion techniques

skills.name: repomix
skills.description: (ePost) Use when you need a full codebase overview, repo summary, or are unfamiliar with a repository structure

skills.name: research
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user asks to research, compare options, find best practices, or investigate a technology

skills.name: review
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "review", "check my code", "is this good", "look at this before I commit", or "suggest improvements" — detects review type (code quality, a11y, or general improvements) and runs the appropriate review

skills.name: scout
skills.description: (ePost) Use when you need to "find files", "search the codebase", "locate a pattern", "explore the repo", or "what exists for X" — searches across platforms using RAG and grep

skills.name: sequential-thinking
skills.description: (ePost) Use when facing a complex problem that needs step-by-step breakdown, branching analysis, or iterative reasoning

skills.name: simulator
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "list simulators", "boot a simulator", "launch the app", "open simulator", or "manage simulators" — controls iOS simulators via XcodeBuildMCP or xcrun simctl

skills.name: skill-discovery
skills.description: (ePost) Use at the START of every task to discover and load relevant skills you don't already have. Detects platform, task type, and domain signals then loads matching skills from the index on demand.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.description: (ePost) Use when a plan has 3+ independent tasks, phases have non-overlapping file ownership, or user says "parallel implementation", "execute all phases" — provides per-task subagent dispatch with two-stage review

skills.name: test
skills.description: (ePost) Use when user says "run tests", "add tests", "check coverage", "write unit tests", or "validate this works" — detects platform and runs the appropriate test suite (Jest, XCTest, JUnit, Espresso)
